raw_feedback: Word count risk: the draft is well beyond 2000–3000 (likely substantially longer), which violates the expected target constraint.
suggested_fix: Bring length into the required 2000–3000 word window (trim repeated diagrams/diagrams sections, consolidate “structure” repetition, reduce duplication across later sections).

raw_feedback: Visual compliance: uses `[Image: ...]` blocks (good), but includes two markdown image references earlier in the TOC/sections only as text; ensure there are no `![alt](url)` syntaxes—none are present, but compliance should be verified in the source.
suggested_fix: Bring length into the required 2000–3000 word window (trim repeated diagrams/diagrams sections, consolidate “structure” repetition, reduce duplication across later sections).

raw_feedback: Reference quality/strength: citations are mostly “Documentation 2023/2024” without author/org + exact document title; several are generic and not precise enough for a strict review standard (e.g., “PyTorch Documentation, 2024” without specific page/title; “Ray Tune Guide, 2023”).
suggested_fix: Bring length into the required 2000–3000 word window (trim repeated diagrams/diagrams sections, consolidate “structure” repetition, reduce duplication across later sections).

raw_feedback: System design checklist requirement: mentions monitoring/observability only lightly (no dedicated section on alerts/metrics for training infra health; little on log aggregation, SLIs/SLOs, or tracing).
suggested_fix: Bring length into the required 2000–3000 word window (trim repeated diagrams/diagrams sections, consolidate “structure” repetition, reduce duplication across later sections).

raw_feedback: Operational concerns gap: does not deeply cover storage/IO bottlenecks, artifact write consistency under concurrent jobs, or security/access control details beyond a brief governance paragraph (and no mention of auditability mechanisms like immutable logs or event sourcing).
suggested_fix: Bring length into the required 2000–3000 word window (trim repeated diagrams/diagrams sections, consolidate “structure” repetition, reduce duplication across later sections).

raw_feedback: Technical specificity: some details are high-level (e.g., “resolve config”, “capture RNG state”) without concrete examples for distributed seeding schemes or sampler state capture mechanisms (e.g., torch DistributedSampler state, resumption samplers, dataloader worker RNG).
suggested_fix: Bring length into the required 2000–3000 word window (trim repeated diagrams/diagrams sections, consolidate “structure” repetition, reduce duplication across later sections).

raw_feedback: Visual compliance: uses `[Image: ...]` blocks (good), but includes two markdown image references earlier in the TOC/sections only as text; ensure there are no `![alt](url)` syntaxes—none are present, but compliance should be verified in the source.
suggested_fix: Upgrade all references to be precise and non-generic: include author/org (e.g., PyTorch Team / Ray Inc. / MLflow / Weights & Biases) and an exact document title/page for each citation (and ensure every cited source is actually named).
